source_rationale: CD69 is a canonical early activation marker for T cells, rapidly upregulated upon TCR stimulation
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine produced by activated T cells involved in immune cell recruitment
Granzyme B is a serine protease in cytotoxic granules, marking activated effector T cells
Interferon-gamma is the hallmark cytokine of activated T cells, especially Th1 and cytotoxic T cells
KLRB1 (CD161) is expressed on activated T cells and NK T cells
IL7R (CD127) marks memory and activated T cell subsets
CD3E is a core component of the TCR complex, a definitive T cell lineage marker
NKG7 is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes and marks activated effector T cells
CXCR4 is upregulated on activated T cells and mediates homing and migration
PTPRC (CD45) is a pan-leukocyte marker essential for T cell receptor signaling
TFAP2B is a transcription factor essential for amacrine cell fate specification in the retina
GAD1 (glutamic acid decarboxylase) marks GABAergic amacrine cells in the retina
TFAP2A is a key transcription factor for amacrine cell differentiation
PAX6 is a master regulator of eye development highly expressed in amacrine cells

GAD2 is the second glutamic acid decarboxylase isoform expressed in GABAergic amacrine cells
